_meta.description: Complete weapon catalog for Samurai Usagi. 130+ weapons across 7 classes.

katana.class_desc: The blade path. Speed and precision. Each cut is a prayer.

weapons.name: Rusty Tantō
weapons.description: A short knife so corroded it might break on bone. Someone threw this away. You picked it up.

weapons.name: Farmer's Sickle
weapons.description: A rice-harvesting sickle. Not a weapon. But it has an edge.

weapons.name: Fish Gutting Knife
weapons.description: Made for filleting, not fighting. Fast as water, fragile as glass. The fishermen keep them sharp enough to shave.

weapons.name: Ashigaru's Katana
weapons.description: Mass-produced blade given to foot soldiers. Functional, unremarkable. A thousand of these were forged the same day.

weapons.name: Soldier's Yari
weapons.description: An ashigaru's spear. Functional, impersonal, made for a hand that follows orders.

weapons.name: Pilgrim's Blade
weapons.description: Given by the Messenger. Its story is not yet written. It hums faintly during prayer.

weapons.name: Mori's Naginata
weapons.description: Commander Mori's polearm. Carrying it feels wrong — or right, depending on who you've become.
weapons.moral_weight: The Accuser will comment on this. SPR -1 if Charity < 3.

weapons.name: Roman Gladius
weapons.description: Short, brutal, efficient. The sword that conquered the world. It still smells of iron and dust.

weapons.name: Nero's Gilded Blade
weapons.description: Taken from the emperor's private collection. Gold inlay, jeweled pommel. Beautiful. Profane. Wielding it corrodes the spirit.
weapons.moral_weight: SPR penalty. The Accuser approves if you equip this.

weapons.name: Desert Scimitar
weapons.description: Curved blade from the Egyptian markets. Fast, light, meant for horseback. It sings through dry air.

weapons.name: Athanasian Sword
weapons.description: A blade carried by guards who protected Athanasius through five exiles. The word 'homoousios' is etched into the fuller.

weapons.name: Crusader Longsword
weapons.description: Heavy cruciform blade. The crossguard forms a cross — by accident or design, no one remembers which.

weapons.name: Saladin's Mercy
weapons.description: A Damascus steel blade given by the Sultan to a Christian knight who showed mercy to prisoners. It cuts clean and heals the conscience.

weapons.name: Templar's Blade
weapons.description: Non nobis, Domine. Not unto us, Lord. The Templars' motto is acid-etched into the blade. It weighs less than it should.

weapons.name: Mongol Dao
weapons.description: Taken from a dead rider. Light, curved, made for speed. It still hums with the wind of the steppe.

weapons.name: Shimabara Katana
weapons.description: Forged by a Christian swordsmith who hid crosses in the grain of the steel. Under magnification, the hamon pattern forms a rosary.

weapons.name: Lepanto Cutlass
weapons.description: Naval blade from the Holy League galleys. Salt-crusted, heavy, made for close quarters on a rocking deck.

weapons.name: Jacobin Saber
weapons.description: Revolution steel. 'Liberté' is stamped on the blade. The liberty it cut was mostly from heads.
weapons.moral_weight: SPR penalty reflects the revolution's spiritual cost.

weapons.name: Resistance Knife
weapons.description: Smuggled into the camp inside a loaf of bread. Small, sharp, desperate.

weapons.name: Blade of Intercession
weapons.description: The Pilgrim's Blade, transformed. It no longer hums — it sings. Every prayer Usagi ever prayed is folded into the steel. It cuts sin, not flesh.
weapons.evolution_condition: Faith ≥ 9, 500+ battles with Pilgrim's Blade, Devotion Grace tier 3

staff.class_desc: The pilgrim's path. Balance and spirit. Moses held one. So did Francis.

weapons.name: Broken Branch
weapons.description: A stick. You picked it up off the ground. It's a stick.

weapons.name: Bamboo Staff
weapons.description: A length of hardened bamboo. Walking stick, weapon, and prayer focus.

weapons.name: Oak Walking Stick
weapons.description: Heavy, knotted oak. More club than staff. It has seen mountains.

weapons.name: Fishing Pole
weapons.description: Flexible bamboo with a broken line. Peter was a fisherman. There are worse role models.

weapons.name: Pilgrim's Walking Staff
weapons.description: Hardened ash wood, wrapped in leather at the grip. Countless miles in its grain.

weapons.name: Franciscan Staff
weapons.description: A walking staff, nothing more. Francis refused all weapons. 'The Lord is my strength.' Deals holy damage based on SPR.

weapons.name: Centurion's Vine Staff
weapons.description: The centurion's symbol of authority. A vine rod, used to discipline legionaries. It commands respect — or fear.

weapons.name: Catacomb Torch-Staff
weapons.description: A staff with a socket for a torch. In the catacombs, light is life. It still smells of pitch and prayer.

weapons.name: Desert Hermit's Crook
weapons.description: Sun-bleached acacia wood shaped by Anthony the Great's own hands. It leans toward prayer.

weapons.name: Athanasius's Crozier
weapons.description: The bishop's pastoral staff. Five times exiled, five times returned. This staff stood against an empire and won.

weapons.name: Crusader Banner Pole
weapons.description: The crossbar of a fallen standard. The banner is gone but the pole remembers what it carried.

weapons.name: Shepherd of Hermas
weapons.description: Named after the early Christian text. A staff that teaches patience. Each impact resonates with a hymn only the faithful hear.

weapons.name: Polish Oak Staff
weapons.description: Cut from a tree split by lightning near the Vistula. Hard as iron, heavy as grief.

weapons.name: Kakure Prayer Staff
weapons.description: A staff carved with hidden Christian symbols — fish, anchors, the Chi-Rho — disguised as decorative patterns. Two hundred years of secret prayers soaked into the wood.

weapons.name: Galley Oar Fragment
weapons.description: Broken oar from a Holy League galley. It pulled Christians through the water. Now it swings through air.

weapons.name: Carmelite Walking Stick
weapons.description: Carried by the Prioress of Compiègne. She leaned on it climbing the scaffold steps. It still stands straight.

weapons.name: Kolbe's Walking Cane
weapons.description: Maximilian Kolbe used this to walk before the starvation bunker. After, he didn't need to walk. He was carried.

weapons.name: Rod of Moses
weapons.description: Not the original — but something like it. A staff that has been prayed over by so many saints it has developed its own gravity. Seas part. Walls fall. The impossible kneels.

cross_blade.class_desc: A blade shaped like the Cross. It cuts and blesses in the same motion.

weapons.name: Broken Cross Shard
weapons.description: A splintered piece of a wooden cross. It cuts, barely. But it remembers what it was.

weapons.name: Cross Dagger
weapons.description: A dagger with a cross-shaped guard. Hidden in the chapel altar stone. Deals holy damage.

weapons.name: Nail-Cross Blade
weapons.description: Three iron nails bound in a cross shape, edges filed sharp. Crude. Desperate. Faithful.

weapons.name: Jesuit Cross-Blade
weapons.description: A processional cross refitted as a weapon. The vertical beam is sharpened. The Jesuit paradox — faith and action, prayer and sword.

weapons.name: Catacomb Cross
weapons.description: Carved from catacomb stone. It was a grave marker before it was a weapon. The dead lend it weight.

weapons.name: Petrine Cross
weapons.description: Shaped like Peter's inverted cross — the cross of humility, not blasphemy. It glows near papal relics.

weapons.name: Nicene Blade
weapons.description: Forged during the Council of Nicaea. 'Homoousios' is hammered into the steel. It burns Arian enemies.

weapons.name: Dominican Truth Blade
weapons.description: 'Veritas' on one side, John 8:32 on the other. Bonus damage against deception and spiritual enemies.

weapons.name: True Cross Relic Blade
weapons.description: A sliver of the True Cross, set into a blade of Damascus steel. Every strike echoes with Calvary. Demonic enemies recoil on contact.

weapons.name: Teutonic Cross-Sword
weapons.description: Heavy blade of the Teutonic Knights. The black cross on white. Discipline over devotion.

weapons.name: Liegnitz Memorial Blade
weapons.description: Forged from the melted swords of those who fell at Liegnitz. It carries their prayers and their defiance.

weapons.name: Kakure Hidden Cross
weapons.description: Looks like a simple farming tool. Unfolds into a cross-shaped blade. Two centuries of hidden faith folded into its hinges.

weapons.name: Rosary Chain Blade
weapons.description: A rosary whose beads are sharpened steel. Each decade draws blood and prayer simultaneously.

weapons.name: Carmelite Scissors
weapons.description: Used to cut cloth for the convent's habits. The Prioress took her last vows with these in her pocket.

weapons.name: Kolbe's Makeshift Cross
weapons.description: Two pieces of wood from the starvation bunker, bound with wire. He fashioned it in the dark so the dying men could see the cross one last time.

weapons.name: Crux Invicta
weapons.description: The Unconquered Cross. Forged from every cross-blade Usagi has ever carried — the nail-cross, the catacomb stone, the Petrine inversion, the Shimabara fold, the starvation wire. They are all here. Every prayer. Every wound. Every refusal to break. It does not glow. It burns.
weapons.evolution_condition: Carried a cross-blade in every book. Faith ≥ 10.

improvised.class_desc: Sickles, hoes, chains, rocks. Whatever is at hand. David killed Goliath with a sling.

weapons.name: Bare Hands
weapons.description: Nothing. You have nothing. God uses what He finds.

weapons.name: River Stone
weapons.description: Smooth, heavy, fits the palm. David killed a giant with one. You're no David. But God doesn't need giants.

weapons.name: Sharpened Hoe
weapons.description: A farming hoe with the edge filed down to something dangerous. Still smells like earth.

weapons.name: Chain Flail
weapons.description: Iron chain with a weighted end. Swings wide, hits hard, asks no questions about honor.

weapons.name: Roof Tile
weapons.description: Ceramic roof tile, edge chipped sharp. Abimelech was killed by a woman dropping one from a tower. Never underestimate masonry.

weapons.name: Iron Pot Lid
weapons.description: Heavy iron lid from a rice pot. Better shield than weapon. Obaa-chan cooked with this for forty years.

weapons.name: Anchor Hook
weapons.description: Bent iron from a fishing boat anchor. Heavy, brutal, and shaped like an early Christian symbol.

weapons.name: Colosseum Chain
weapons.description: Chain from the arena gates. It held lions. Now it holds your rage.

weapons.name: Potter's Wheel Disk
weapons.description: Heavy clay disk. Thrown like a discus, it breaks bones. 'Behold, like clay in the potter's hand, so are you in My hand.'

weapons.name: Tent Stake
weapons.description: Iron tent peg from a Crusader camp. Jael killed Sisera with one. Improvised weapons have biblical precedent.

weapons.name: Siege Rubble
weapons.description: A chunk of wall from Acre. The castle fell; this piece didn't. It carries the weight of a kingdom.

weapons.name: Mongol Bola
weapons.description: Weighted stones on leather cords. Spin and release. The Mongols used these to tangle horses' legs.

weapons.name: Shimabara Pitchfork
weapons.description: A farmer's pitchfork from the Shimabara Rebellion. The peasants fought with these against samurai swords. Some of them won.

weapons.name: Galley Anchor Chain
weapons.description: Heavy chain that held a Holy League galley in place at Lepanto. Wrapped around the fist, it is devastating.

weapons.name: Guillotine Bolt
weapons.description: A bolt from the guillotine mechanism. You took it so the machine couldn't work. It didn't help. They had more bolts.
weapons.moral_weight: Carrying this is an act of defiance — and futility.

weapons.name: Barbed Wire Wrap
weapons.description: Barbed wire from the camp fence, wrapped around a stick. Every swing draws your blood too. ATK scales with missing HP.

weapons.name: Jawbone of Samson
weapons.description: Not literally. But something with the same energy — the most absurd, impossible, shouldn't-work-but-does weapon in the game. God's sense of humor made manifest. It hits like a building falling on someone.

fist.class_desc: No weapon. The body itself becomes prayer. The ascetic's path.

weapons.name: Bare Hands
weapons.description: Nothing but skin and bone and will. The ascetic's weapon.

weapons.name: Cloth Hand Wraps
weapons.description: Strips of cotton wound around the knuckles. Protects your hands, not your enemies.

weapons.name: Leather Fighting Gloves
weapons.description: Hardened leather over the knuckles. The grip is stiff. The impact is not.

weapons.name: Iron Knuckles
weapons.description: Cast iron wrapped around the fingers. Crude. Effective. The opposite of elegant.

weapons.name: Prayer Bead Wraps
weapons.description: Rosary beads woven into hand wraps. Every punch is a Hail Mary. Every block is an Our Father.

weapons.name: Roman Caestus
weapons.description: Gladiator's boxing glove — leather straps reinforced with iron and lead. One punch breaks ribs.

weapons.name: Desert Father's Wraps
weapons.description: Linen wraps blessed by Anthony the Great. They have been prayed in for decades. The fists they cover deal holy damage.

weapons.name: Monk's Discipline Bands
weapons.description: Iron bands worn tight around the wrists as penance. They hurt the wearer more than the enemy. That's the point.

weapons.name: Gauntlet of Godfrey
weapons.description: Chain mail gauntlet from the Crusader commander. It shook the hand of both saint and sinner.

weapons.name: Mongol Horsehide Gloves
weapons.description: Thick horsehide, bone-reinforced. Mongol warriors wrestled in these. Fast, brutal, no wasted motion.

weapons.name: Shimabara Rebel Wraps
weapons.description: Cloth torn from a rebel's banner, wrapped around fists that refused to bow. +10% ATK when HP < 50%.

weapons.name: Galley Slave Chains
weapons.description: Broken chains still clasped to wrist-irons. The slave is free. The chains remain. They remember every stroke of the oar.

weapons.name: Carmelite Discipline
weapons.description: The knotted cord of self-mortification. Each blow dealt to an enemy also deals 5% to Usagi. But the SPR scaling is immense.

weapons.name: Survivor's Fists
weapons.description: No wraps. No gloves. Just hands that have dug graves, carried the dying, and prayed over the dead. They are stronger than any weapon.

weapons.name: Hands of the Saint
weapons.description: Not a weapon. Usagi's own hands, transformed by a lifetime of prayer. They glow faintly. They heal what they touch and break what they strike. The stigmata have opened.

spear.class_desc: The soldier's weapon. Reach, discipline, formation.

weapons.name: Sharpened Stick
weapons.description: A pointed stick. The first weapon ever made. You are continuing a proud tradition.

weapons.name: Bamboo Spear
weapons.description: Hardened bamboo with a fire-sharpened tip. The weapon of peasant rebellions across Asia.

weapons.name: Ashigaru Yari
weapons.description: Standard-issue spear for foot soldiers. Three meters of ash wood and an iron point. It does what it's told.

weapons.name: Fishing Harpoon
weapons.description: Barbed iron on a wooden shaft. Made for tuna, used for survival. The barbs make wounds that don't close.

weapons.name: Roman Pilum
weapons.description: The javelin that conquered the Mediterranean. Thrown, it bends on impact so it can't be thrown back. Held, it's a passable spear.

weapons.name: Praetorian Spear
weapons.description: The emperor's personal guard carried these. Gold-banded, perfectly balanced. It has tasted imperial blood.

weapons.name: Desert Sarissa
weapons.description: Long pike from the Egyptian garrison. Unwieldy alone, devastating in formation.

weapons.name: Crusader Lance
weapons.description: Cavalry lance with a cruciform guard. Designed for the charge — one impact, one prayer, one life.

weapons.name: Holy Lance (Replica)
weapons.description: Modeled after the Lance of Longinus. Not the original — that was lost centuries ago. But faith doesn't care about provenance.

weapons.name: Mongol Cavalry Lance
weapons.description: Light lance from a Mongol rider. Designed for speed, not formation. Strike and withdraw.

weapons.name: Polish Winged Hussar Lance
weapons.description: The weapon of Poland's legendary cavalry. The wooden wings on the hussar's back whistled in the charge — psychological warfare. The lance itself is pure physics.

weapons.name: Shimabara Bamboo Pike
weapons.description: The rebels at Shimabara faced samurai with bamboo. They held the castle for months. Faith is its own fortification.

weapons.name: Naval Boarding Pike
weapons.description: Short, heavy pike for boarding actions at Lepanto. Designed for the cramped quarters of a galley deck.

weapons.name: Revolutionary Pike
weapons.description: The mob's weapon of choice. Crude iron on rough wood. It carried heads through the streets of Paris.
weapons.moral_weight: Carrying this makes certain NPCs hostile.

weapons.name: Camp Fence Stake
weapons.description: Wooden stake pulled from the camp fence. It held barbed wire. Now it holds defiance.

weapons.name: Spear of Destiny
weapons.description: The spear that pierced Christ's side. From the wound flowed blood and water — death and life in one thrust. It is the most dangerous and the most sacred weapon in existence. It does not obey the wielder. The wielder obeys it.

bow.class_desc: The watchman's path. Distance and patience. A single arrow at the right moment.

weapons.name: Makeshift Sling
weapons.description: A strip of leather and two cords. It throws stones. It's how David did it.

weapons.name: Hunting Shortbow
weapons.description: Small bamboo bow for hunting birds and rabbits. Usagi tries not to think about the irony.

weapons.name: Yumi Half-Bow
weapons.description: Traditional Japanese asymmetric bow, cut down for foot use. The grip sits below center — physics, not aesthetics.

weapons.name: Ashigaru Longbow
weapons.description: Full-sized yumi from a soldier's kit. Powerful but slow to draw. Volley fire, not precision.

weapons.name: Roman Composite Bow
weapons.description: Horn, wood, and sinew layered together. The technology of empires compressed into a curve.

weapons.name: Arena Crossbow
weapons.description: Mechanical crossbow from the arena. Slow to reload but devastating. The crowd loved it.

weapons.name: Egyptian Recurve Bow
weapons.description: Desert-crafted recurve. The dry heat keeps the laminations tight. Accurate at distance.

weapons.name: Saracen Horse Bow
weapons.description: Short, powerful bow designed for mounted archery. Fast draw, fast flight, fast kill.

weapons.name: English Longbow
weapons.description: Six feet of yew. The weapon that won Agincourt. A lifetime to master. One second to kill.

weapons.name: Mongol Composite Bow
weapons.description: The weapon that conquered the world. Horn, birch, and ibex sinew. Fires from horseback at full gallop.

weapons.name: Kakure Bamboo Bow
weapons.description: Bamboo bow with Christian symbols carved into the grip. The arrows whisper the Orasho as they fly.

weapons.name: Lepanto Naval Crossbow
weapons.description: Heavy crossbow used on the galley decks. It punched through Ottoman shields at fifty paces.

weapons.name: Converted Musket Bow
weapons.description: A musket stock rigged with a bow mechanism after the powder ran out. Improvisation meets desperation.

weapons.name: Resistance Slingshot
weapons.description: Surgical tubing and a Y-shaped stick. The resistance used these for silent attacks. David's technology, refined.

weapons.name: Bow of the Watchman
weapons.description: An arrow of light that finds its mark across any distance, through any deception, past any defense. The bow is not a physical object — it is the shape Usagi's hands make when he draws back a prayer and releases it.
